meta.voice: Brendan Franks. Mix of mention, engagement, and reply-frame tweets for natural afternoon-long pacing. Each tweet has a specific account or 2-3 accounts tagged. Each leads with a number or named entity.
meta.rules: Under 280 chars. Lead with the data point or named entity. The @ tag appears naturally. End with offer / question / invitation. Reply tweet has the link.

posts.text:
ACC Commissioner Nick Myers' on-record line at @KJZZPhoenix on the APS heat-disconnect: "I refuse to tell utilities they have to provide power to people that do not pay their bills."

Three days. 99 degrees. Korman, age 82, found dead six days later.
posts.reply:
We have the AG settlement language, the ME findings, and the named ACC vote. Anyone covering the 2019 prior decedent that triggered the original moratorium?

cc @AZAGMayes @AARPArizona @kdaviswho

posts.text:
ASU Prep's FY24 IRS 990: $5.46M operating loss on $82.4M revenue. The FY21 audit at the Arizona Department of Education filing portal discloses a related-party building lease.

$11.5M ROU asset. Through September 2032. The counterparty name lives in Note 9.
posts.reply:
Public records path under ARS 39-121. We mapped the federal funds schedule and the ALA precedent:

azlawnow.com/investigations/asu-prep-related-party-lease/

cc @AZCIRorg @ChalkbeatNews @lilyaltavena

posts.text:
DOJ-EDNY Walgreens settlement covers conduct August 2012 through March 2023. Arizona lost 1,659 people to opioids in the latest 12-month surveillance window. Which AZ Walgreens stores filled the prescriptions covered by the settlement?

The list sits in the EDNY federal docket.
posts.reply:
Records that close the gap: the consent decree exhibits, DEA Phoenix Federal Register entries, and AZ Board of Pharmacy meeting minutes:

azlawnow.com/investigations/walgreens-350m-arizona-opioid-stores/

cc @ShatterproofHQ @AZAGMayes

posts.text:
Across the street from the proposed 1,500-bed Surprise federal detention facility: Rinchem stores chlorine, hydrofluoric acid, and fluorine. The EPA Risk Management Plan was filed three weeks before the federal warehouse purchase on January 23, 2026.

The RMP doesn't contemplate detention.
posts.reply:
Maryland TRO landed March 11. NJ March 20. Michigan March 24. AZ April 24. Eleventh Circuit's Friends of the Everglades opinion is the test, AZ meets it cleanly:

azlawnow.com/investigations/arizona-mayes-ice-surprise-detention-lawsuit/

cc @ACLUArizona @FIRRPAZ

posts.text:
Arizona's Private Postsecondary Education Board licensed 237 career schools and hit 37 with adverse actions in FY25. Federal 60-percent rule under 34 CFR 668.22 creates structural pressure to keep students enrolled past the aid waterline.

Corinthian and ITT discharges set the precedent.
posts.reply:
Full list of named adverse actions + the federal rule mechanics + the Corinthian / ITT analog:

azlawnow.com/investigations/arizona-career-schools-37-adverse-actions/

cc @ProtectBorrowers @USDOEd @studentdebt_org

posts.text:
Arizona school districts restrained students 4,200+ times in a single school year. The practice concentrates on students with IEPs and 504 plans. ARS 15-105 governs restraint and seclusion in AZ schools. The federal IDEA private right of action applies.

The data is public. Coverage isn't.
posts.reply:
We pulled the per-district breakdown + the statutory hooks + the IDEA enforcement frame:

azlawnow.com/investigations/arizona-school-restraint-data/

cc @ndrn @AAPD @DisRightsAZ

posts.text:
Arizona's pedestrian fatality rate runs nearly 2x the national average. The worst corridors map cleanly against ADOT and MAG road-design choices. FHWA's Proven Safety Countermeasures menu shows what redesigns work.

Which AZ cities have picked them up? The data answers that.
posts.reply:
We mapped the corridors against the design decisions and the Vision Zero adoption gaps:

azlawnow.com/investigations/arizona-pedestrian-deaths-road-design/

cc @StreetsblogUSA @StrongTowns @smartgrowthusa

posts.text:
Tempe's own grant filings flag pavement failure near ASU. Arizona's 180-day Notice of Claim clock under ARS 12-821.01 hits injured riders. ABOR publicly says ASU can't accept service of claims.

Fong v. City of Phoenix (2024) requires expert testimony on bike-lane defects.
posts.reply:
We laid out the 180-day clock, the service-of-process gap, and the Fong evidentiary frame:

azlawnow.com/investigations/tempe-asu-pavement-180-day-claim-clock/

cc @StreetsblogUSA @BikeLeague

posts.text:
The Mayes / DHS / ICE complaint pleads NEPA, INA, and Clean Air Act RMP claims. The Eleventh Circuit's Friends of the Everglades opinion held the post-2023 NEPA threshold of "more than minimal federal funding and control."

Arizona's $70M purchase + $300M contracts clears it cleanly.
posts.reply:
We mapped the Florida case vs the Surprise facts. The Maryland TRO is the precedent:

azlawnow.com/investigations/arizona-mayes-ice-surprise-detention-lawsuit/

cc @LawFareBlog @aclu_immigrants

posts.text:
60% of nursing-home sexual-abuse victims have dementia. 80% of perpetrators are caregivers. Only 30% of incidents reach law enforcement. A Mesa Grand Court Senior Living staffer was arrested April 21, 2026. Family caught it on video.

HB2228 advances in the AZ Senate.
posts.reply:
We mapped the data, the statutory frame, and what HB2228 actually changes:

azlawnow.com/investigations/grand-court-mesa-elder-abuse-hb2228/

cc @AARP @ConsumerVoiceLT @LeadingAge

posts.text:
ASHRAE 52.2 tests filter media in a sealed apparatus, NOT the housing seal in the field. ASHRAE 241-2023 says air bypassing an unsealed filter performs as a lower MERV. EPA tightened PM2.5 NAAQS to 9 micrograms in 2024.

No Arizona district publishes a PM2.5 reading.
posts.reply:
We pulled the standards text, the EPA NAAQS tightening, and the Maricopa PM10 non-attainment context:

azlawnow.com/investigations/arizona-schools-merv-13-filter-bypass/

cc @ASHRAEnews @SOSArizona

posts.text:
Arizona disciplined 325 educators in 2024. Sexual misconduct: 36% of 1,876 cases since 2012. Maricopa County drove 55.6% of cases between Jan 2023 and Dec 2024. Voluntary surrender is the dominant outcome at 39%.

The data has been public.
posts.reply:
We walked the ARS 12-514 12-year SOL and the ARS 12-821.01 180-day NOC clocks plus six theories of district recovery:

azlawnow.com/investigations/arizona-325-educator-discipline-2024/

cc @ChalkbeatNews @the74
